vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1934: verification gate drives agents through up to 10 retry cycles on an unfixable check (no operator escalation, invisible burn)

plan.title: Verification gate: cap retries at 3 and escalate to the operator instead of looping silently
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: The verification gate (src/lib/cloister/verification-runner.ts) caps retries at VERIFICATION_MAX_CYCLES=10 and, on reaching the cap, sets verificationStatus:'skipped' and lets the work advance to review/merge with a broken check. Every failure writes a feedback file that tells the agent 'Do NOT stop ... keep working'. There is no no-progress detection and no operator escalation, so an agent that broke a check it cannot fix grinds through up to 10 full-suite runs (~28s each) of invisible token burn. The documented PAN-174 behaviour (CLAUDE.md:309, docs/SPECIALIST_WORKFLOW.md:560 - 'after 3 consecutive failures, verification is bypassed') disagrees with the code, and the doc even names a nonexistent file (verification-gate.ts).
narratives.Proposal: Lower the cap to 3 (matching the docs). Add a pure decideVerificationDisposition() seam and a shared handleVerificationFailure() helper used by all four failure branches. On the escalation condition (cap reached, OR the same gate failing on >=2 consecutive cycles via a new persisted verificationLastFailedCheck) the gate pauses+stops the agent (async stopAgent/setAgentPaused), marks the workspace stuck (markWorkspaceStuck - the existing needs-you surface), and writes a STOP feedback file, returning a new outcome:'escalated'. The pre-run guard returns 'escalated' (never the old silent 'skipped'), and verificationSatisfied() treats 'escalated' as not-satisfied so escalated work cannot ride the merge gate. The three callers in workspaces.ts stop advancing on 'escalated'. Docs and the dashboard are aligned (cap 3, escalation surfaced).
narratives.NonGoals:
- Per-test-name no-progress tracking (gate-name granularity only).
- Changing the quality_gates command set or what counts as a failing check.
- The untracked pi/kimi cost-accounting sibling issue.
- A configurable per-project cap (the cap stays a hardcoded constant).
- Auto-resuming an escalated agent (operator intervenes via the existing unstick/unpause flow).
- A new pipeline event type or a new needs-you UI surface beyond the existing stuck surface.

autoDecisions.rationale: The issue allows '<=3'; 3 aligns the constant with the already-documented number (CLAUDE.md:309) - the least-surprise choice.

autoDecisions.summary: No-progress threshold = 2 (same gate on 2 consecutive cycles escalates).
autoDecisions.rationale: Catches the incident (a repeatedly-failing test gate) one cycle before the hard cap while staying conservative.

autoDecisions.summary: No-progress granularity = gate name, not individual test name.
autoDecisions.rationale: Robust and matches the incident (the 'test' gate kept failing); per-test parsing of vitest output is fragile and out of scope.

autoDecisions.summary: Reuse existing primitives via async/Effect variants: markWorkspaceStuck + setAgentPaused + stopAgent.
autoDecisions.rationale: Matches pan pause semantics and the existing operator surfaces; the async variants are server-safe (the runner runs in the dashboard server).

autoDecisions.summary: Apply escalation to all four failure branches, not only the test gate.
autoDecisions.rationale: Sync-target, quality-gate, vbrief-conflicts and vbrief-ac are the same hazard class; one shared helper is the root-cause fix, not a half-fix.

autoDecisions.summary: Escalated blocks merge; pre-run guard returns 'escalated' not 'skipped'.
autoDecisions.rationale: The entire bug is broken work advancing silently; escalated work must be held until a human intervenes.

autoDecisions.summary: verificationLastFailedCheck persisted on the review-status record (DB cache); tolerates cache-rebuild loss.
autoDecisions.rationale: It is a within-sequence hint; on loss the logic falls back to the hard cap. Avoids journaling churn.
Visibility comes from verificationStatus:'escalated' plus the existing stuck/status_changed surface; avoids contract churn.

items.title: Persist verificationLastFailedCheck on the review-status record

narrative.Action: Add an optional verificationLastFailedCheck field to the ReviewStatus record so the gate can detect when the same check fails on consecutive cycles. Add `verificationLastFailedCheck?: string` to the ReviewStatus interface in src/lib/review-status.ts (near :52); add a `try { db.exec('ALTER TABLE review_status ADD COLUMN verification_last_failed_check TEXT'); } catch {}` line to the migration block in src/lib/database/schema.ts (alongside the existing review_status ALTER TABLE statements at :1084-1143); and wire the column into the INSERT column list + value binding and the rowToReviewStatus SELECT mapper in src/lib/database/review-status-db.ts (:44-116, :399-456). The field is written/read via setReviewStatusSync/getReviewStatusSync.

items.title: The ReviewStatus interface in src/lib/review-status.ts contains an optional verificationLastFailedCheck string field.
The schema migration creates the verification_last_failed_check TEXT column on review_status inside a try/catch, matching the existing review_status migration block.

Calling setReviewStatusSync(issueId, { verificationLastFailedCheck: 'test' }) then getReviewStatusSync(issueId) returns verificationLastFailedCheck equal to 'test' (the field round-trips through the DB mapper).
Add the escalated verification status and block it from the merge gate

narrative.Action: Introduce 'escalated' as a verificationStatus value and make it block merge. Add the literal "escalated" to VerificationStatusValue in packages/contracts/src/types.ts:39; add 'escalated' to the verificationStatus union in src/lib/review-status.ts:52; and change verificationSatisfied() (src/lib/review-status.ts:122) to return false when verificationStatus is 'escalated' as well as 'failed'. No DB schema change is needed - verification_status is already a TEXT column. Rebuild packages/contracts (npm run build in packages/contracts) after the literal change so the frontend type picks it up.

items.title: VerificationStatusValue in packages/contracts/src/types.ts contains the literal escalated.
verificationSatisfied({ verificationStatus: 'escalated' }) returns false.
mergeGateEligibility() with verificationStatus 'escalated' returns eligible false (escalated work is rejected from the merge gate).
A unit test asserts both that 'failed' still blocks and that 'escalated' now blocks verificationSatisfied and mergeGateEligibility.
Lower the cap to 3 and add the pure decideVerificationDisposition seam

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/verification-runner.ts change `export const VERIFICATION_MAX_CYCLES = 10` to `= 3`, and add `export const NO_PROGRESS_REPEAT_THRESHOLD = 2`. Add and export a pure function decideVerificationDisposition({ newCycleCount, failedCheck, lastFailedCheck }) returning { disposition: 'escalate' | 'retry', reason: string }: escalate when newCycleCount >= VERIFICATION_MAX_CYCLES, OR when failedCheck === lastFailedCheck && newCycleCount >= NO_PROGRESS_REPEAT_THRESHOLD; otherwise retry. Add a co-located unit test (tests/cloister/verification-disposition.test.ts). This function has no I/O and no delays - it needs no timers.

items.title: verification-runner.ts contains exported constants VERIFICATION_MAX_CYCLES set to 3 and NO_PROGRESS_REPEAT_THRESHOLD set to 2.
decideVerificationDisposition returns disposition escalate when newCycleCount >= 3 (hard cap), regardless of failedCheck.
decideVerificationDisposition returns escalate when failedCheck equals lastFailedCheck and newCycleCount >= 2 (no-progress), and returns retry when newCycleCount equals 1.
decideVerificationDisposition returns retry when newCycleCount equals 2 but failedCheck differs from lastFailedCheck (a moving target waits for the hard cap).
Escalate (pause + stuck) on the failure path instead of looping; make the pre-run guard sticky

metadata.acJustification: This is the single cohesive behavioral change (escalation handler + the four converted failure branches + the sticky pre-run guard); its six ACs each pin a distinct observable behavior of that one change and splitting it would leave an inconsistent intermediate (some branches escalating, others still looping).

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/verification-runner.ts: (1) add 'escalated' to the VerificationRunnerOutcome union as { outcome: 'escalated'; failedCheck: string; cycleCount: number; maxCycles: number }. (2) Extract a shared async helper handleVerificationFailure({ issueId, workspacePath, failedCheck, summary, retryFeedbackBody }) used by all four failure branches (sync-target :224, quality-gate :352, vbrief-conflicts :399, vbrief-ac :433). It reads prev = getReviewStatusSync(issueId), computes newCycleCount = (prev?.verificationCycleCount ?? 0) + 1, calls decideVerificationDisposition({ newCycleCount, failedCheck, lastFailedCheck: prev?.verificationLastFailedCheck }), and persists verificationCycleCount + verificationLastFailedCheck. On 'retry' it keeps today's behaviour (verificationStatus:'failed', writeFeedbackFile with the retry body, messageAgent, return { outcome:'failed', ... }). On 'escalate' it sets verificationStatus:'escalated'; writes a feedback file whose body instructs the agent to STOP (it must NOT contain the phrase 'do NOT stop'); calls markWorkspaceStuck(issueId, 'verification_unfixable', { failedCheck, cycleCount: newCycleCount }); then setAgentPaused(agent-<issueId-lowercase>, reason, true) and stopAgent(agent-<issueId-lowercase>) via Effect.runPromise (the async primitives from agents.ts - NEVER stopAgentSync); any messageAgent is best-effort in try/catch and runs before stopAgent; returns { outcome:'escalated', failedCheck, cycleCount:newCycleCount, maxCycles:VERIFICATION_MAX_CYCLES }. (3) Repurpose the pre-run guard (:192-199): when prev.verificationStatus === 'escalated' OR (prev?.verificationCycleCount ?? 0) >= VERIFICATION_MAX_CYCLES, return { outcome:'escalated', ... } without running quality gates (replaces the old { outcome:'skipped' }). Update tests/cloister/verification-runner.test.ts: change the circuit-breaker test to expect 'escalated'; add markWorkspaceStuck to the review-status.js mock and setAgentPaused+stopAgent to the agents.js mock; add a no-progress test (same 'test' failure on cycle 2 -> escalated) and a first-failure test (cycle 1 -> 'failed', no pause/stop).

items.title: VerificationRunnerOutcome includes an outcome 'escalated' variant carrying failedCheck, cycleCount and maxCycles, returned when escalation fires.

When the same gate fails on the second consecutive cycle, runVerificationForIssue returns outcome escalated and the runner calls markWorkspaceStuck, setAgentPaused, and stopAgent (asserted via mocks).
The escalation feedback file body instructs the agent to STOP and contains no occurrence of the phrase Do NOT stop.
The pre-run guard returns outcome escalated (not skipped) and does not call runQualityGates when verificationCycleCount >= VERIFICATION_MAX_CYCLES or verificationStatus is already escalated.
A single first-time failure (cycle 1, no prior failed check) returns outcome failed and does NOT call setAgentPaused or stopAgent.
The verification-runner pause path imports only the async stopAgent and setAgentPaused from agents.js, and a grep over the file produces no match for stopAgentSync.
Stop advancing on outcome escalated at the three verification call sites

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/routes/workspaces.ts handle outcome === 'escalated' at all three runVerificationForIssue call sites, mirroring the existing 'failed' handling but without advancing. Review-dispatch (the :3870 block): on escalated, completePendingOperation with an escalation note and return without spawning the review role. pan review request (the :4296 block): on escalated, return jsonResponse({ success:false, escalated:true, failedCheck, cycleCount, maxCycles, message:'Verification escalated to operator - agent paused' }) and do not dispatch review. Post-rebase merge-verify (the :5685 block): treat escalated the same as failed (set mergeStatus:'failed', readyForMerge:false, do not proceed to merge).

items.title: The review-dispatch path does not spawn the review role when runVerificationForIssue returns outcome escalated.
The pan review request path returns a response with escalated true and does not dispatch review when verification escalates.
The post-rebase merge-verify path sets mergeStatus failed and readyForMerge false and does not merge when verification returns escalated.
Align the docs with cap=3 and escalation, and fix the stale file reference

metadata.acJustification: A docs-correction bead; its observable behaviors are the corrected statements and the corrected file reference.

narrative.Action: Update the CLAUDE.md 'Verification Gate (PAN-174)' section (:304-309) to state that after 3 consecutive failures the gate escalates (pauses the agent and marks the workspace stuck for operator attention) and blocks the work from merge, rather than being 'bypassed'. Update docs/SPECIALIST_WORKFLOW.md:560 the same way and replace the nonexistent 'src/lib/cloister/verification-gate.ts' reference with 'src/lib/cloister/verification-runner.ts'.

items.title: CLAUDE.md states the gate escalates (pauses the agent and marks the workspace stuck) after 3 cycles and no longer says verification is bypassed.
docs/SPECIALIST_WORKFLOW.md contains a reference to src/lib/cloister/verification-runner.ts and no longer contains the string verification-gate.ts.
Each doc contains the number 3 as the documented cap, matching the VERIFICATION_MAX_CYCLES constant.
Surface the escalated verification status in the dashboard and align the default cap

narrative.Action: Render the new 'escalated' verification status distinctly in the cockpit and align the frontend cap default. In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Stage/cockpit/ReviewVerificationCard.tsx make the Build-gate Step render a distinct non-green (failed-like) state for verificationStatus 'escalated' and show the stuck reason when present. Change DEFAULT_VERIFICATION_MAX_CYCLES from 10 to 3 in src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/ZoneCOverviewTabs/ReviewPipelineSection.tsx. Ensure the frontend verificationStatus type (workspace-types.ts / queries.ts) accepts 'escalated' (it derives from the contracts literal, so rebuilding contracts in the escalated-status bead covers it). Follow the dashboard style guide (no decorative color; amber/red are 'a human must act').

items.title: ReviewVerificationCard renders the Build-gate Step in a distinct non-green (failed-like) state when verificationStatus equals escalated.
When an issue is escalated and stuck, the cockpit shows the stuck reason to the operator.
ReviewPipelineSection.tsx contains DEFAULT_VERIFICATION_MAX_CYCLES set to 3.
